name: Cartopy (Cartographic Projections and Mapping)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3-Clause); UK Met Office / SciTools

performance.notes: Matplotlib-based; rendering speed depends on map complexity and tile downloads

agent_readiness.notes: Cartographic projections and geospatial visualization for Matplotlib. import cartopy.crs as ccrs. import cartopy.feature as cfeature. ax = plt.axes(projection=ccrs.PlateCarree()). ax.add_feature(cfeature.COASTLINE). ax.add_feature(cfeature.BORDERS). ax.gridlines(). ax.set_extent([-180, 180, -90, 90]). Projections: PlateCarree, Mercator, Mollweide, Robinson, LambertConformal. Background tiles: Stamen, OSM. Transform: transform=ccrs.PlateCarree() in plot calls. Scatter: ax.scatter(lons, lats, transform=ccrs.PlateCarree()). NetCDF/xarray data visualization.

gotchas: Complex installation — depends on GEOS and PROJ libraries (conda recommended)
transform= parameter required on all plot calls — easy to forget
Online tile downloads can fail — cache or use local tiles for reproducibility
No type annotations — poor IDE support
Slow for complex maps with many features
best_when: Weather/climate data on projected maps (PlateCarree, Mercator, polar)
Adding coastlines, borders, gridlines to scientific figures
avoid_when: Interactive web maps (use folium or plotly geo)
When conda is not available (pip install is unreliable)
summary: Cartopy provides cartographic projections and geospatial visualization for Matplotlib. plt.axes(projection=ccrs.PlateCarree()). ax.add_feature(cfeature.COASTLINE). 30+ projections (Mercator, Robinson, Mollweide). Background tiles. ax.scatter(..., transform=ccrs.PlateCarree()). BSD-3 by UK Met Office. Best for scientific map figures (weather, climate, oceanography). Use conda for installation. transform= required on all plot calls.
